message:
Improve BTree cursor robustness, add flat combining concurrency primitive, and expand parser expression handling

- Enhance BTree cursor seek and navigation with improved bounds checking
  and edge case handling for empty/single-element pages
- Add flat_combining.rs implementing a flat combining concurrency
  primitive for batching concurrent operations on shared data structures
- Improve compat_persist module with additional error context and
  recovery path handling
- Expand SQL expression parser to handle additional expression forms
  and improve operator precedence handling
- Fix value type conversion edge cases in fsqlite-types
- Update beads issue tracker with frankenlab integration and flat
  combining correctness tracking
- Add bd-2g5.7 frankenlab integration test and bd-2g5.8 flat combining
  correctness test with corresponding verification scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add a dedicated integration test (fsqlite-harness path) plus scripts/verify_bd_2g5_5_cross_process.sh for reproducible CI/local validation. Require detailed structured logging so failures can be triaged by crash point, process role, and invariant class.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue structured acceptance_criteria field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Harness test covers deterministic multi-process crash cycles, slot reclamation, seqlock torn-read checks, and Left-Right linearizability assertions with reproducible seeds. 2. Unit/property tests validate crash-injection state transitions, invariant evaluation logic, and false-positive resistance. 3. Deterministic end-to-end verification script (scripts/verify_bd_2g5_5_cross_process.sh) runs full scenario matrix and emits replay commands plus artifact bundles. 4. Structured logging/metrics include trace_id/run_id/scenario_id/process_role/crash_point/timing and actionable failure diagnostics, with CI schema-conformance checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:58.644073275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T04:54:08.050345383Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T04:54:08.050321248Z","close_reason":"Validated cross-process harness/tests and fixed verify_bd_2g5_5_cross_process.sh path/replay parsing; rch check+tests pass and verifier JSON reports matrix_complete=true schema_errors=0","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","e2e","ev-gated","frankensqlite","test"]}
 {"id":"bd-2g5.6","title":"Asupersync Cx capability context threading","description":"Asupersync Cx integration: Thread franken_kernel Cx (capability context) through all FrankenSQLite operations. TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId propagation. Structured concurrency scope for transactions. Cancel-correctness for in-flight queries. Tracing: uses Cx.trace_id for all spans. Log level: WARN on missing Cx propagation. Metric: fsqlite_cx_propagation_failures_total counter.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Cx propagated through all async operations. 2. TraceId available in all spans. 3. Cancel-correctness: no resource leaks on cancellation. 4. Zero Cx propagation failures in test suite.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:51.433784488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:16.110863960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:16.110845736Z","close_reason":"Cx trace/decision/policy ID propagation complete — 15 tests added, all Connection operations wired","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","concurrency","cross-crate","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2g5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2g5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2g5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2g5.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.829211014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3046,"issue_id":"bd-2g5.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation: Cx Capability Context Threading (BrownCliff)

### Summary
Added TraceId, DecisionId, and PolicyId propagation to the Cx capability context.
Wired through Connection operations so every pager/WAL/btree call carries
trace context for end-to-end observability.

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-types/src/cx.rs`**
- Added `trace_id: u64`, `decision_id: u64`, `policy_id: u64` fields to `Cx<Caps>` struct
- All derivation methods propagate IDs: `clone()`, `restrict()`, `scope_with_budget()`,
  `cleanup_scope()`, `create_child()`, `retype()`
- New builder methods: `with_trace_context()`, `with_decision_id()`, `with_policy_id()`
- New accessors: `trace_id()`, `decision_id()`, `policy_id()`
- 12 new tests covering all propagation paths

**`crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`**
- Added `root_cx: Cx` field to `Connection` struct — initialized with `next_trace_id()` at open
- Added `root_cx: Cx` field to `PreparedStatement` — inherited from Connection at prepare time
- Added `op_cx()` helper: derives per-operation context with fresh `next_decision_id()`
- Replaced all 15 production `Cx::new()` calls with `self.op_cx()` / `conn.op_cx()`
- Methods updated: close_internal, ensure_autocommit_txn_mode, resolve_autocommit_txn,
  with_pager_write_txn, execute_begin, execute_commit, execute_rollback,
  execute_savepoint, execute_release, pragma_wal_checkpoint, pragma_database_header,
  apply_journal_mode_to_pager, reload_memdb_from_pager (in open)
- 3 new integration tests

### Test Results
- 46 cx tests passing (12 new, 34 existing)
- 828 fsqlite-core tests passing (3 new, net +1 improvement over baseline)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, fmt
","created_at":"2026-02-17T23:46:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2g5.6.1","title":"Cx propagation telemetry bridge and regression gate","description":"Extend observability plumbing so bd-2g5.6 can prove Cx propagation guarantees: add explicit telemetry in crates/fsqlite-observability and connection/transaction paths for propagation failures, trace linkage, and cancellation cleanup outcomes. Wire metric emission and structured WARN diagnostics on missing/invalid Cx propagation. Add regression tests and deterministic replay checks to ensure propagation failures are detected and surfaced automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue structured acceptance_criteria field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Telemetry bridge defines and emits Cx propagation counters/histograms (including propagation-failure counter) with stable metric names and CI-visible export. 2. Unit/property tests validate propagation instrumentation across success/failure/cancellation paths and verify counter monotonicity plus trace-link integrity. 3. Deterministic end-to-end scenario script exercises async connection and transaction flows, proving propagation visibility and cancellation-safe cleanup with replayable commands. 4. Structured logging emits trace_id/run_id/scenario_id/policy_id/decision_id and actionable propagation-failure context, with CI log-schema conformance enforcement.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.110114538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:14:11.753553680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T09:14:11.436041758Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","ev-gated","frankensqlite","metrics","observability"],"comments":[{"id":2998,"issue_id":"bd-2g5.6.1","author":"PearlCastle","text":"Claimed. Starting observability plumbing for Cx propagation telemetry.","created_at":"2026-02-14T09:09:37Z"},{"id":2999,"issue_id":"bd-2g5.6.1","author":"PearlCastle","text":"Added CxPropagationMetrics to fsqlite-observability with 6 AtomicU64 counters: propagation_successes/failures, cancellation_cleanups, trace_linkages, cx_created, cancel_propagations. Tracing: span 'flat_combine' with fields batch_size, combiner_thread, wait_time_us. Log level: DEBUG batch execution, INFO periodic contention stats.  {"id":"bd-2gn9","title":"OpLog Generator: Seeded concurrent write workloads (commutative + contended)","description":"## Goal
Implement a generator that emits OpLogs for selected fixtures/presets.

## Design Notes
- Prefer **structured ops** -> render SQL late so both executors use identical SQL.
- Use commutative operations for deterministic final logical state (disjoint key ranges / disjoint tables).
- For contended presets, specify retry policy (backoff, max retries) so executors behave comparably.

## Acceptance
- `--seed` produces identical OpLog bytes across runs.
- Can generate both:
  - commutative workload (expected to converge without conflicts)
  - contended workload (expected to trigger busy/retry)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:53.776699576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.251719265Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.251705649Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of existing E2E suite tasks; superseded by canonical bd-z0xm subgraph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","workload"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gn9","depends_on_id":"bd-1an0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2174,"issue_id":"bd-2gn9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task title: OpLog Generator: Seeded concurrent write workloads (commutative + contended) 
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing e2e harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gx0","title":"Bench & Speedup: benchmark matrix + fairness + reporting","description":"## Goal

Make it trivial to measure and communicate FrankenSQLite performance wins on real DB fixtures.

This is not just "run Criterion"; we want:

- a matrix runner (engine × fixture × workload × concurrency × repeats)
- strict fairness knobs (PRAGMAs + environment capture)
- robust summaries (percentiles, throughput, speedup)
- human and machine outputs

## Outputs

- A benchmark config schema (preset names, axes, defaults).
- A benchmark runner (CLI) that produces:
  - JSONL records for each run
  - aggregate summaries
  - Markdown report(s)
- Speedup computation and clear presentation of:
  - throughput curves vs concurrency
  - latency percentiles
  - conflict/busy rates (esp. for MVCC)

## Acceptance

- Running the same matrix twice yields statistically similar results (with warmup + repeats).
- Reports clearly show FrankenSQLite vs baseline, with speedup ratio and context.
- Results are easy to consume in CI logs and in a local TUI.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:23:51.811819731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:47:20.891744801Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:47:20.891729192Z","close_reason":"Duplicate plan: use canonical RealDB epic bd-2als (with detailed sub-epics/tasks + acceptance criteria).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2gzw","title":"§10.4-10.8 Name Resolution + Planning + CodeGen + VDBE + Coroutines","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §10.4-§10.8 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-18zh — §10.3-10.4 AST Node Types + Name Resolution Algorithm
- bd-q0oz — §10.5 Query Planning: Cost Model, Index Selection, Join Ordering
- bd-1mtt — §10.6 Code Generation: AST to VDBE Bytecode Compilation
- bd-gird — §10.7-10.8 VDBE Instruction Format + Coroutines

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:07:31.543142759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:32:53.265892730Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:39:58.401142118Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Superseded by finer-grained bd-q0oz (§10.5) + bd-1mtt (§10.6) + bd-gird (§10.7-10.8)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gzw","depends_on_id":"bd-1ik","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2gzw","depends_on_id":"bd-1x55","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2175,"issue_id":"bd-2gzw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §10.4 Name Resolution

Transforms raw AST identifiers into fully-resolved references.

**Table alias binding:** FROM table AS alias -> binding alias -> table_schema. Column refs use either name or alias.

**Column reference resolution (t.col):** (1) Search current scope aliases for t, (2) verify col in table schema, (3) if t omitted, search all FROM tables — exactly one match resolves, multiple = "ambiguous column name" error.

**Star expansion:** SELECT * -> all columns all tables. SELECT t.* -> all columns of t.

**Subquery scoping:** Stack of scopes. Inner scopes reference outer (correlated subqueries). Column refs checked innermost-first, walking outward.

## §10.5 Query Planning

**Cost model (page reads):**
- Full scan: N_pages(table)
- Index range: log2(N_pages(index)) + selectivity * N_pages(index) + selectivity * N_pages(table)
- Index equality: log2(N_pages(index)) + log2(N_pages(table))
- Covering index: log2(N_pages(index)) + selectivity * N_pages(index)
- Rowid lookup: log2(N_pages(table))

Uses ANALYZE stats (sqlite_stat1/stat4) when available, else heuristic estimates.

**Index usability:** Equality (=, leftmost column), Range (>, BETWEEN, rightmost constraint), IN (expanded probes), LIKE (constant prefix).

**Join ordering:** Bounded best-first search (beam search) matching C SQLite's NGQP (wherePathSolver). mxChoice: 1 for single-table, 5 for 2-table, 12/18 for 3+ tables. Complexity: O(mxChoice * N^2) bounded beam.

## §10.6 Code Generation (Illustrative VDBE Traces)

**SELECT:** Init -> Transaction -> Variable -> OpenRead -> SeekRowid -> Column -> ResultRow -> Close -> Halt.
**INSERT:** Init -> Transaction -> OpenWrite -> NewRowid -> Variable -> MakeRecord -> Insert -> Close -> Halt.
**UPDATE:** Init -> Transaction -> Variable -> OpenWrite -> NotExists -> Column -> Copy -> MakeRecord -> Insert REPLACE -> Close -> Halt.
**DELETE:** Init -> Transaction -> Variable -> OpenWrite -> NotExists -> Delete -> Close -> Halt.

**Concurrent-mode note:** OP_NewRowid MUST allocate via snapshot-independent RowId allocator (S5.10.1.1), not snapshot-visible max(rowid).

## §10.7 VDBE Instruction Format

VdbeOp { opcode: Opcode (u8), p1/p2/p3: i32, p4: P4 enum, p5: u16 }. P4 variants: None, Int32, Int64, Real, String, Blob, FuncDef, CollSeq, KeyInfo, Mem, Vtab, Table, Subprogram.

**Jump resolution:** Label system: emit_label() -> Label handle, resolve_label(label, addr) patches refs. All labels resolved before execution.

**Register allocation:** Numbered from 1. Sequential via alloc_reg()/alloc_regs(n). Temporary registers pooled and returned. Persistent registers (results, cursor positions) held for statement lifetime.

## §10.8 Coroutines

Subqueries and CTEs use VDBE coroutine mechanism: InitCoroutine sets r_yield, Yield swaps PCs between outer query and coroutine body. EndCoroutine performs final swap. Allows on-demand row production without materializing entire result. Layout varies by compilation phase (WITH RECURSIVE, subquery flattening may differ).
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:07:31Z"}]}

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# CI verification gate for bd-2g5.7: FrankenLab deterministic testing integration
+# Validates deterministic scheduling with concurrency primitives.
+set -euo pipefail
+
+echo "=== bd-2g5.7: FrankenLab Integration Verification ==="
+
+# Run the harness integration tests
+cargo test --package fsqlite-harness --test bd_2g5_7_frankenlab_integration -- --nocapture 2>&1
+
+echo ""
+echo "[GATE PASS] bd-2g5.7 FrankenLab integration — all tests passed"

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# CI verification gate for bd-2g5.8: Flat Combining correctness tests
+# Validates sequential batching under contention with no lost updates.
+set -euo pipefail
+
+echo "=== bd-2g5.8: Flat Combining Correctness Verification ==="
+
+# Run the harness integration tests
+cargo test --package fsqlite-harness --test bd_2g5_8_flat_combining_correctness -- --nocapture 2>&1
+
+echo ""
+echo "[GATE PASS] bd-2g5.8 flat combining correctness — all tests passed"
